serialization: UTF-8 JSON with LF canonical line endings

incident_reference.name: 0289 NE DARLENE 3
incident_reference.role: incident reference and AOI derivation only

service.organization: Monitoring Trends in Burn Severity Program (U.S. Geological Survey and USDA Forest Service)
service.copyright_text: Monitoring Trends in Burn Severity Project (U.S. Geological Survey and USDA Forest Service)

queries.purpose: enumerate every current 2024 occurrence record and locally check Darlene name, known incident reference, and AOI coordinates
query 2024 occurrence features intersecting the BurnLens discovery envelope that contains the frozen final AOI
query all-years occurrence features intersecting the same envelope to rule out a year-layer or naming artifact

findings.interpretation: The current official occurrence service does not expose a Darlene 3 record in the BurnLens AOI. This is an availability finding, not evidence that no burn scar exists.

rights_review.basis: USGS states that USGS-authored or produced data and information are considered U.S. public domain, while some non-USGS site materials may carry separate rights. This record retains factual public service metadata and citations only.

revision_policy: Re-query before later use. MTBS publishes approximately quarterly, and a revised fire replaces the original distributed dataset.

source_guidance.role: mapping threshold, missing-fire reasons, quarterly revision behavior, fire-level products, scale, and interpretation limits
current MTBS purpose, assessment timing, availability, audience, and data-access route

source_guidance.organization: USDA Forest Service Enterprise Map Services
source_guidance.role: current official occurrence and burned-area service layer inventory
USGS public-domain rule, exceptions for third-party materials, credit, and identifier limitations
